description: Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) is an XML-based vector image format for two-dimensional graphics defined by the W3C. SVG files are text-based and may begin with an optional UTF-8 BOM (EF BB BF), followed by an XML declaration (<?xml version="1.0"...) and then the <svg> root element with namespace and viewport attributes. SVGZ files are gzip-compressed SVG. Since SVG is text-based, header parsing examines the BOM, XML declaration bytes, and opening SVG tag.
author: WPFHexaEditor Team

alternateSignatures.description: XML declaration start (<?xml) — SVG files commonly begin with an XML declaration before the <svg> tag
UTF-8 BOM followed by XML declaration
UTF-8 BOM followed directly by <svg> tag

functions.detectBOM(0): Detect Byte Order Mark at the beginning of the file to determine encoding (UTF-8 EF BB BF, UTF-16 LE FF FE, UTF-16 BE FE FF)
functions.countLines(): Count the total number of lines in the SVG text file
functions.detectLineEnding(): Detect the line ending style used in the file (CRLF, LF, or CR)
functions.formatFileSize(): Format the file size as a human-readable string with appropriate unit suffix

blocks.name: Byte Order Mark (BOM)
blocks.description: Optional UTF-8 Byte Order Mark (EF BB BF). When present, these 3 bytes precede all other content and indicate UTF-8 encoding. Not all SVG files include a BOM; its presence shifts all subsequent offsets by 3 bytes. UTF-16 BOMs (FF FE or FE FF, 2 bytes) are also possible but rare for SVG.

blocks.name: XML Declaration Opening
blocks.description: The opening '<?xml' sequence (hex 3C 3F 78 6D 6C) that begins the XML declaration processing instruction. Most well-formed SVG files include this declaration before the <svg> element. Offset may be 0 or 3 (if BOM is present).

blocks.name: XML Declaration Separator
blocks.description: Mandatory whitespace character (0x20 space) separating '<?xml' from the version attribute. Per XML specification, at least one whitespace character is required here.

blocks.name: XML Version Attribute
blocks.description: The version attribute of the XML declaration, typically 'version="1.0"' (14 bytes). XML version 1.0 is used by virtually all SVG files. Version 1.1 exists but is extremely rare in practice.

blocks.name: XML Encoding Region
blocks.description: Region containing the optional encoding attribute, typically ' encoding="UTF-8"'. Captures the encoding declaration that specifies the character encoding used in the document. UTF-8 is the most common encoding for SVG files.

blocks.name: XML Declaration Close
blocks.description: The closing '?>' sequence of the XML declaration processing instruction. This marks the end of the XML declaration and the beginning of the document content (typically whitespace followed by the <svg> element).

blocks.name: SVG Root Tag Opening
blocks.description: The opening '<svg ' sequence (hex 3C 73 76 67 20) of the SVG root element. The space after 'svg' is required before attributes. Offset varies depending on XML declaration length and line ending style; 39 assumes a typical '<?xml version="1.0"?>\n<svg ' structure.

blocks.name: SVG Namespace Region
blocks.description: Region expected to contain the SVG namespace declaration 'xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"'. This mandatory attribute identifies the document as SVG and enables correct namespace resolution by parsers and renderers.

blocks.name: SVG Viewport Attributes Region
blocks.description: Region containing SVG viewport attributes such as viewBox, width, and height. These attributes define the coordinate system and dimensions of the SVG canvas. Actual content and offsets vary per file.

blocks.name: BOM Detection Result
blocks.description: Determines whether a Byte Order Mark is present at the start of the file. A BOM indicates encoding and shifts all subsequent content offsets.

blocks.name: Encoding Detection
blocks.description: Determines the document encoding from the BOM bytes. If no BOM is present, UTF-8 is assumed as it is the default for XML documents.

blocks.name: BOM Presence Check
blocks.trueLabel: This SVG file contains a Byte Order Mark (BOM) — all content offsets are shifted accordingly
blocks.falseLabel: No BOM detected — file content starts directly with XML declaration or <svg> tag
blocks.description: Checks whether a BOM is present, which affects the byte offsets of all subsequent content in the file.

blocks.name: Compressed SVG Detection
blocks.trueLabel: This is a compressed SVGZ file (gzip-compressed SVG)
blocks.falseLabel: This is a plain-text SVG file (uncompressed)
blocks.description: Checks whether the SVG file is gzip-compressed (SVGZ). Compressed SVG files begin with the gzip magic number (1F 8B) instead of XML/SVG text content.

blocks.name: Detected Encoding
blocks.description: Character encoding detected from BOM or assumed as UTF-8 (the XML default)

blocks.name: Has BOM
blocks.description: Whether a Byte Order Mark is present at the beginning of the file

blocks.name: Line Count
blocks.description: Total number of lines in the SVG text file

blocks.name: Line Ending Style
blocks.description: Line ending convention used: CRLF (Windows), LF (Unix/macOS), or CR (classic Mac)

blocks.name: Is Compressed (SVGZ)
blocks.description: Whether this is a gzip-compressed SVGZ file

UseCases: Interactive web graphics and data visualizations
